name: Nairobi
description: Capital city and economic hub of Kenya. Known as the 'Green City in the Sun' with vibrant nightlife, business centers, and diverse entertainment options.

name: Mombasa
description: Coastal city and major port. Famous for beautiful beaches, historical sites like Fort Jesus, and Swahili culture. Tourism hotspot.

name: Kiambu
description: Part of Nairobi Metropolitan Region. Known for coffee farming, affluent suburbs, and proximity to the capital.

name: Nakuru
description: Home to Lake Nakuru National Park (flamingos) and Menengai Crater. Major agricultural and tourism county.

name: Kisumu
description: Third largest city in Kenya. Located on the shores of Lake Victoria. Known for fishing industry and vibrant lakeside culture.

name: Uasin Gishu
description: Home to Eldoret town, known as the 'City of Champions' for producing world-class athletes. Major agricultural region.

name: Meru
description: Agricultural county known for miraa (khat), coffee, and tea. Rich cultural heritage of the Meru people.

name: Machakos
description: Part of Nairobi Metropolitan. Known for sandstone hills, vibrant markets, and growing urban centers.

name: Kakamega
description: Home to Kakamega Forest, the only tropical rainforest in Kenya. Known for sugarcane farming and Luhya culture.

name: Kisii
description: Densely populated county known for soapstone carvings, banana farming, and the Gusii people's rich culture.

name: Kilifi
description: Coastal county with popular tourist destinations like Malindi and Watamu. Known for beautiful beaches and marine parks.

name: Kericho
description: Tea-growing capital of Kenya. Famous for vast tea plantations and cool climate.

name: Bungoma
description: Agricultural county known for sugarcane farming. Home to the famous bullfighting tradition.

name: Murang'a
description: Central Kenya county known as the 'heartland of the Kikuyu'. Famous for coffee and fresh produce.

name: Nyeri
description: Home to Mount Kenya and Aberdare Ranges. Rich agricultural land and historical significance.

name: Kajiado
description: Maasai county bordering Tanzania. Known for wildlife, Maasai culture, and growing urban centers.

name: Narok
description: Home to Maasai Mara National Reserve, famous for the Great Wildebeest Migration.

name: Kwale
description: Coastal county with Diani Beach and Shimba Hills National Reserve. Known for pristine beaches and coral reefs.

name: Tana River
description: County along the Tana River, known for irrigation agriculture and wildlife reserves.

name: Lamu
description: Archipelago county with UNESCO World Heritage site Lamu Old Town. Known for Swahili architecture and culture.

name: Taita-Taveta
description: Known for Tsavo National Parks, wildlife conservation, and scenic Taita Hills.

name: Garissa
description: Northeastern county and commercial hub. Known for Somali culture and Tana River delta.

name: Wajir
description: Largest county in Northeastern Kenya. Known for livestock keeping and pastoralist communities.

name: Mandera
description: Border county with Ethiopia and Somalia. Known for livestock trade and pastoralist lifestyle.

name: Marsabit
description: County with Marsabit National Park and Lake Turkana. Known for diverse ethnic communities.

name: Isiolo
description: Gateway to Northern Kenya. Known for wildlife conservation and diverse cultures.

name: Tharaka-Nithi
description: Agricultural county known for miraa, coffee, and fruit farming along the Tana River.

name: Embu
description: Agricultural county on the slopes of Mount Kenya. Known for coffee, tea, and macadamia nuts.

name: Kitui
description: Semi-arid county known for livestock keeping, mining, and traditional crafts.

name: Makueni
description: Eastern Kenya county known for fruit farming (mangoes, oranges) and vibrant markets.

name: Nyandarua
description: Agricultural county in the Aberdare Ranges. Known for potato farming and dairy production.

name: Kirinyaga
description: County on the slopes of Mount Kenya. Known for rice farming in Mwea and coffee production.

name: Turkana
description: Largest county in Kenya. Known for Lake Turkana, oil discoveries, and Turkana people's rich culture.

name: West Pokot
description: County with Cherangani Hills. Known for pastoralism and vibrant cultural festivals.

name: Samburu
description: Home to Samburu National Reserve. Known for the colorful Samburu culture and wildlife.

name: Trans Nzoia
description: Breadbasket of Kenya. Known for large-scale wheat and maize farming.

name: Elgeyo-Marakwet
description: County with Kerio Valley. Known for athletics champions and scenic landscapes.

name: Nandi
description: Home of champions. Known for producing world-class athletes and tea farming.

name: Baringo
description: County with Lake Baringo and Lake Bogoria. Known for geothermal activity and wildlife.

name: Laikipia
description: Wildlife conservation county. Known for private ranches and community conservancies.

name: Bomet
description: Agricultural county known for tea farming and dairy production in the South Rift region.

name: Vihiga
description: Smallest county in Kenya. Known for sugarcane farming and Luhya cultural heritage.

name: Busia
description: Border county with Uganda. Known for cross-border trade and fishing on Lake Victoria.

name: Siaya
description: Home to Lake Victoria beaches. Known as the home of the first African US President's father.

name: Homa Bay
description: County on Lake Victoria shores. Known for fishing industry and Ruma National Park.

name: Migori
description: County with Macalder gold mines. Known for sugarcane farming and Lake Victoria fishing.

name: Nyamira
description: Agricultural county known for tea, bananas, and pyrethrum farming in Gusii highlands.
